key_insight: A coupled LSTM + physics-informed GNN can reconstruct non-linear, history-dependent local stress fields by separating time-path encoding from spatial field recovery.
engineering_relevance: This enables fast, mesh-agnostic surrogate evaluation of elasto-plastic microstructures, reducing the cost of multi-scale simulations while preserving field-level accuracy.
method: LSTM encodes macroscopic stress-strain loading history into a compact latent state
Physics-informed GNN/MeshGraphNet reconstructs spatial stress fields on the microstructure mesh
Training uses a weighted data-loss plus discrete divergence equilibrium penalty with linear warm-up
evidence_signal: Trained on 10,000 non-proportional loading paths for a periodic plate-with-a-hole and achieved about 3 orders of magnitude speedup over finite elements, with 1.9% cumulative error on sequences twice as long as training.
watch_item: The equilibrium penalty weighting and hidden-state interpretation may be sensitive to training setup, and generalization should still be validated for different materials, boundary conditions, and stronger nonlinearity.
paper_title: Non-linear mechanical field reconstruction coupling recurrent neural networks with physics-informed graph neural networks
